notes: Curated catalyst list for BTC/ETH/XRP/SUI plus crypto-relevant macro. Each entry must carry a source_url and a confidence rating. Macro events (FOMC/CPI/NFP) are merged in at render-time from js/data/macro_events.js to avoid duplication.

events.title: SUI Gasless Stablecoin Transfers launched (historical)
events.desc: Gasless stablecoin transfers launched on Sui Mainnet on May 20, 2026 (Address Balances account system). Supports USDC, FDUSD, AUSD, USDsui, SuiUSDe, USDB, USDY. NOTE: this catalyst has already passed — kept for historical reference. SUI rallied +7.11% on the day. Previous calendar entry listed May 25 ('soft midpoint of week of May 23') — that was a mirage; the real launch was Tue May 20 per blog.sui.io.
events.why_it_matters: Use as historical reference point. The 'gasless stablecoin' rail removes the #1 onboarding friction for SUI payment dApps; future re-rating around dApp adoption announcements is the live signal, not the upgrade date itself.

events.title: XRPL fixCleanup3_1_3 amendment activates
events.desc: XRPL on-chain amendment: NFT offer deletion behaviour + permission settings fix. Minor maintenance amendment but on-chain confirmable.
events.why_it_matters: Not a price catalyst on its own, but confirms regular protocol activity. Useful as a confidence anchor that XRPL governance is functioning normally heading into the CLARITY Act news cycle.
events.bull_scenario: Amendment passes smoothly with >80% validator consensus, confirming healthy on-chain governance. Adds a quiet positive backdrop for XRP ahead of the CLARITY Act unified-draft announcement.
events.bear_scenario: Amendment fails or stalls on low validator participation. Rare, but a governance hiccup would hand critics a talking point and add friction to the broader XRP regulatory narrative — modest negative.

events.title: CME Bitcoin May options expiry
events.desc: Last Friday of May — monthly CME BTC options expire. Max-pain and gamma-flip strikes drive late-week pinning behavior. Stacks with the CME crypto 24/7 trading rollout on the same session.
events.why_it_matters: Largest US-regulated BTC options venue. Heavy strikes ±5% of spot routinely act as magnets in the final 24h.
events.bull_scenario: Max-pain strike sits above current price. Dealer gamma flips long as spot approaches — accelerates the move up. Price pins at or above max-pain into 16:00 ET close, setting a clean weekly close.
events.bear_scenario: Max-pain sits below current price. Dealers are short gamma and forced to sell into weakness. Heavy put-wall below acts as a magnet — BTC gets dragged down to pin lower strikes into settlement.

events.title: CME crypto futures + options flip to 24/7 trading
events.desc: All CME crypto futures and options contracts switch from 23/5 to continuous 24/7 trading. Eliminates the weekend gap that historically caused Sunday-night/Monday-morning repricing events.
events.why_it_matters: Structural change to how regulated crypto derivatives interact with spot. Removes the weekly basis dislocation; tightens arb between CME and offshore venues. Should compress weekend tail-risk premium across the entire CME crypto suite.
events.bull_scenario: Smooth rollover. Liquidity providers expand weekend quoting. Weekend volatility compresses, basis stays tight. Treated as a quiet plumbing upgrade — neutral to mildly bullish for institutional flows.
events.bear_scenario: Weekend liquidity is thin in the first sessions. Wider spreads + low depth create disorderly basis moves that bleed into spot. One-off shake-out in low-cap CME contracts (SUI/AVAX) before market makers adapt.

events.title: CME SUI futures launched (historical)
events.desc: First US-regulated futures contract for SUI listed on CME on May 4, 2026. Standard contract = 50,000 SUI, micro = 5,000 SUI, cash-settled. First block trades printed May 6 between G-20 Group and FalconX. NOTE: this catalyst has already passed — kept for historical reference. The post-launch tape sold off heavily.
events.why_it_matters: The CME SUI launch was a sell-the-news event, not a sustained bid. Use as a reference point — do not position for it again. Any future 'institutional access' narrative for SUI should be discounted against this post-launch drawdown.

events.title: SUI monthly token unlock
events.desc: Scheduled monthly cliff release from the SUI vesting schedule (Series A/B + early contributor tranches). Historically prints ~1-2% of circulating supply.
events.why_it_matters: SUI tends to see sell pressure in the 48-72h around unlock. Confluence with weak macro can deepen the dip; confluence with strong tape often gets bought.
events.bull_scenario: Macro tape is strong into unlock (BTC above key weekly level, no negative FOMC surprise). Unlock volume gets absorbed quickly — dip stays under 2% and is bought within 24h, confirming structural demand. VC address on-chain shows no movement.
events.bear_scenario: Weak macro coincides with unlock. Early contributor wallets show outflows on-chain in the T-48h window. Selling compounds — SUI drops 5–10% in the 72h window. Watch for the $1.00 psychological level as first support.

events.title: Ethereum Glamsterdam upgrade (ePBS + BALs)
events.desc: Next ETH hard fork after Fusaka. Targets H1/H2 2026 — exact date not yet set. Headliners: enshrined Proposer-Builder Separation (ePBS) and Block-level Access Lists (BALs). NOTE: a prior calendar entry incorrectly bundled EIP-7594 PeerDAS into Glamsterdam — PeerDAS shipped with Fusaka (mainnet Dec 3, 2025), not Glamsterdam. Date_precision lowered to quarter until a coordinated devnet/testnet schedule is published on ethereum.org.
events.why_it_matters: ePBS reduces validator-builder centralization and BALs unlock parallel transaction execution. Bullish narrative reset for ETH if it lands clean; risk-off catalyst if delayed (Shanghai 2023 precedent: –8% on delay announcement). Watch All Core Devs (ACD) calls for testnet dates.
events.bull_scenario: Devnets stabilize; testnet activation window confirmed. ETH/BTC ratio breaks its 3-month downtrend on scaling-narrative re-acceleration. ETH reprices toward $3,000+.
events.bear_scenario: Client teams hit a late-stage consensus bug and slip Glamsterdam to 2027. Developer confidence dents; ETH/BTC continues underperforming.

events.title: CME Bitcoin Q2 quarterly futures + June options expiry
events.desc: Last Friday of June — CME quarterly futures roll AND monthly options expiry land on the same session. Largest single-day open-interest reset of the quarter.
events.why_it_matters: Quarterly + monthly stacked. Settlement-day volatility plus roll-related basis dislocations. Watch the daily close 16:00 ET.
events.bull_scenario: Max-pain sits above spot. Large call-wall concentration above current price forces dealers to buy delta heading into settlement. Quarterly roll sees institutions re-enter at higher strikes — net bullish re-positioning. Clean weekly close above the quarterly open.
events.bear_scenario: Max-pain prints below spot. Dealers are net short gamma and sell into any weakness. Roll activity pushes basis negative. Overleveraged longs get squeezed into the 16:00 ET fix — BTC drops 3–5% into settlement, resetting positioning for Q3.

events.title: SUI monthly token unlock
events.desc: Scheduled monthly cliff release from the SUI vesting schedule.
events.why_it_matters: Same playbook as the June unlock — watch the 48-72h window around the date. Pattern recognition across three consecutive months builds the clearest edge.
events.bull_scenario: June unlock was absorbed cleanly, establishing a pattern of buy-the-dip behavior. July unlock sees the same — any T-48h dip below key support gets bid quickly. Confirms the unlock 'discount window' playbook is working.
events.bear_scenario: June unlock caused a sustained selloff that hasn't recovered by July 1. Second consecutive month of heavy unlock selling compounds — VC / contributor wallets show continued distribution. SUI breaks the Jun low.

events.title: EthCC[9] — Ethereum Community Conference
events.desc: Annual ETH ecosystem conference. Typically early July. Major project announcements, EIP progress updates, L2 launches frequently timed to the week.
events.why_it_matters: ETH and L2 narrative bursts often kick off in the EthCC week. Watch ENS, ARB, OP, LINEA sympathy moves.
events.bull_scenario: Multiple L2 teams announce mainnet upgrades at EthCC. A major dApp (DeFi or consumer) launches on a leading L2 to a live audience. ETH/BTC ratio catches a bid — ETH leads the field for the week.
events.bear_scenario: Conference is announcement-light. No new L2 milestones, EIP progress updates underwhelm. Classic 'buy the rumour, sell the news' — ETH sells off into the conference week after a pre-conference run-up.

events.title: Bitcoin 2026 Conference — Las Vegas (historical)
events.desc: Bitcoin 2026 ran Apr 27–29, 2026 at The Venetian, Las Vegas. NOTE: this catalyst has already passed — kept for historical reference. Previous calendar entry listed 'Nashville July 2026' which was a mirage: Nashville is the Bitcoin 2027 venue (Jul 15–17, 2027), not 2026.
events.why_it_matters: Historical reference. The next forward-looking BTC conference catalyst is Bitcoin 2027 in Nashville (Jul 15–17, 2027) — add it when within the 90-day calendar window.

events.title: CME Bitcoin July options expiry
events.desc: Last Friday of July — monthly CME BTC options expire.
events.why_it_matters: Standard monthly pin/gamma risk. Lower OI than the quarterly but still meaningful for late-week price action.
events.bull_scenario: Max-pain above current price. Positive dealer gamma pins BTC at or above max-pain through the close, delivering a clean monthly candle close. Bullish setup heading into August.
events.bear_scenario: Max-pain below current price. Negative dealer gamma amplifies downside into settlement. July closes below a key monthly level, resetting the technical picture bearish into August.

events.title: SUI monthly token unlock
events.desc: Scheduled monthly cliff release from the SUI vesting schedule.
events.why_it_matters: Three-in-a-row through the quarter. By August you have three data points — the pattern clarity is highest here. Either the buy-the-dip playbook has a clean track record or the selling has been persistent.
events.bull_scenario: Two prior unlocks (Jun, Jul) were both absorbed cleanly and bought. Institutional buyers have now established a pattern of stepping in at the unlock discount. August unlock dip gets bid within hours — SUI holds above the Q2 high.
events.bear_scenario: Unlock selling has been consistent across all three months. August adds a third consecutive negative data point — the market interprets it as a structural overhang. Break below the Q2 low opens deeper retracement toward $0.85–$0.90.

events.title: Institutional 13F filing deadline (Q2 2026)
events.desc: 45 days after quarter-end — 13F filings disclose institutional holdings of spot BTC/ETH ETFs (IBIT, FBTC, ETHA, etc.).
events.why_it_matters: Confirms whether sovereign wealth funds, pensions, and hedge funds added or trimmed. Headlines around individual holders (e.g. 'Norway SWF added X to IBIT') drive intraday spikes.
events.bull_scenario: New sovereign wealth funds, pension funds, or major endowments revealed as first-time IBIT/FBTC buyers. Cumulative institutional AUM in spot ETFs crosses a headline threshold. Media cycle drives retail FOMO and re-accelerates inflows.
events.bear_scenario: Major prior holders (e.g. Goldman, hedge funds) show reduced positions. No new large institutional entrants. 'Institutional adoption plateau' narrative spreads — ETF inflow deceleration becomes a headwind for the Q3 narrative.

events.title: CLARITY Act unified draft / floor scheduling
events.desc: Banking Committee cleared the CLARITY Act 15-9 on May 14, 2026. Polymarket odds at 73% (vs 62% pre-markup). Next milestone is the joint Banking + Agriculture committee unified draft — assigning SEC vs CFTC jurisdiction over digital assets.
events.why_it_matters: The next XRP catalyst is NOT another vote — it's the announcement of a unified draft merging Banking (digital assets) and Agriculture (commodity/DeFi jurisdiction) versions. The merge is where the ethics provision (Trump conflict-of-interest language) gets resolved. Watch Senate.gov for joint committee filings or leadership press conferences.
events.bull_scenario: Banking and Ag chairs release a joint unified draft with the ethics provision resolved. Floor vote is scheduled with 60-vote path confirmed. XRP re-rates as a commodity with no SEC overhang — XRP +15–25% on announcement day. BTC/ETH/SUI all catch a regulatory tailwind.
events.bear_scenario: Ethics provision deadlock kills the merge — neither committee accepts the other's language. Floor scheduling is indefinitely delayed past Q3. XRP gives back the Polymarket premium it has been carrying (currently ~11% above pre-73% levels). Potential –10–15% reversion.

events.title: XRP spot ETF flow milestone watch
events.desc: Cumulative net inflows into US-listed XRP spot ETFs. May 2026 was the strongest month on record per daily report ($1.39B cumulative). Q3 milestone watch: $3B / $5B cumulative thresholds.
events.why_it_matters: ETF flow data is the cleanest gauge of institutional demand. Sustained $50M+ daily inflows = trend confirmation; first negative-flow week = warning sign.
events.bull_scenario: Daily inflows sustain above $50M. Cumulative AUM crosses the $3B headline threshold — media cycle amplifies. A new major issuer (Fidelity, Invesco) sees a weekly record. XRP breaks above $3.00 resistance on flow confirmation.
events.bear_scenario: First week of net outflows — early adopters take profits, no new institutional buyers to replace them. Daily run-rate falls below $20M. ETF demand narrative reverses from tailwind to headwind. XRP consolidates and underperforms BTC/SUI.
